purpose: Generate heat through electrical resistance for furnace heating

inputs.name: heating_current
inputs.description: Electrical current flowing through heating elements

outputs.name: heat_transfer
outputs.description: Heat flux generated by heating elements

states.name: electrical_resistance
states.description: Heating element resistance that varies with temperature

states.name: corrosion_level
states.description: Degradation from chemical corrosion, iron oxide scale, and reactive atmospheres

events.name: heating_element_failure
events.description: Heating element must be replaced when out of order

events.name: electrical_bridge_formation
events.description: Vapours form condensates from salts or oxides creating electrical bridges

events.name: lead_fracture
events.description: Stresses can fracture the lead at the element weld

parameters.name: power_to_temp_factor
parameters.description: Conversion factor from electrical power to temperature rise

parameters.name: heat_transfer_efficiency
parameters.description: Efficiency of converting electrical power to heat transfer

parameters.name: element_failure_threshold
parameters.description: Corrosion level threshold for element failure

parameters.name: bridge_formation_corr_threshold
parameters.description: Corrosion level threshold for bridge formation

parameters.name: lead_fracture_threshold
parameters.description: Corrosion level threshold for lead fracture

state_transition_logic: electrical_resistance approaches base_resistance * (1 + temp_coefficient * (element_temperature - reference_temp)) using first-order lag with resistance_response_coeff
element_temperature approaches heating_current^2 * electrical_resistance * power_to_temp_factor using first-order lag with thermal_response_coeff
All states clamped to their respective min_value and max_value bounds
event_logic: electrical_bridge_formation occurs when corrosion_level < bridge_formation_corr_threshold AND element_temperature > bridge_formation_temp_threshold
assumptions: Resistance varies linearly with temperature according to temperature coefficient
Corrosion rate increases with operating temperature
Heat transfer output is proportional to electrical power and reduced by corrosion
Element temperature responds to electrical power input with first-order dynamics
Bridge formation requires both high temperature and significant corrosion
All degradation mechanisms are captured through corrosion_level state
